steps.title: Locate Himalayan balsam in the table
steps.content: Scan the “Neighboring species” column to find the row containing Himalayan balsam. This row identifies which target species it was paired with and where to find the corresponding effect value.

steps.title: Read the effect value directly from that row
steps.content: Himalayan balsam appears in the second row, paired with marsh woundwort, and its effect value is 0.7905. The other three values—−0.3238 (leafy spurge with Lewis flax), 0.4729 (Canadian wood betony with mayapple), and −3.2570 (which does not appear in the table)—belong to different neighboring species or are not present in the data. That matches choice A — A is correct.
